ranked_movies.title: The Lobster
ranked_movies.reason: Highly discussed as a mind-bending, hilarious, and horrifying dystopian film that sparked a major conversation about unconventional love stories and led to numerous recommendations for similar films.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as the benchmark for a 'traumatizing' yet artistically valid film experience, generating a massive thread seeking similar intense movies.

ranked_movies.title: The Act of Killing
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times across different posts as a shocking, mind-blowing documentary that challenges viewers' perceptions of history and morality.

ranked_movies.title: Three Identical Strangers
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently recommended as a shocking and mind-blowing documentary that surprises viewers with its unbelievable true story.

ranked_movies.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
ranked_movies.reason: Directly recommended as the logical next step after 'The Lobster' and discussed for its unsettling, thought-provoking nature from the same director.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a chilling 'comedy' with intersecting storylines that deeply disturbs viewers, fitting the request for films that traumatize with intention.

ranked_movies.title: Irreversible
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in detail for its intentionally nauseating and traumatic filmmaking techniques, making it a prime recommendation for those seeking a severely impactful experience.

ranked_movies.title: The Jinx
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a mind-blowing documentary series with shocking revelations, particularly in true crime.

ranked_movies.title: Mysterious Skin
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a film that 'shook me to my core' and is extremely dark, fitting the request for genuinely good movies that severely affect the viewer.

ranked_movies.title: Kids
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for having an ending 'burned in my memory' and recommended as a dark, impactful film about NYC teenagers and HIV.

ranked_movies.title: Logan
ranked_movies.reason: Praised as an unexpected masterpiece within the superhero genre, with brutal action and great character development, sparking a discussion about films that exceeded expectations.

ranked_movies.title: Galaxy Quest
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times as a film that far exceeded expectations, with perfect casting and hilarious moments, becoming a cult classic.

ranked_movies.title: The Emperor's New Groove
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as an unexpected Disney masterpiece that earned its love through video rentals despite little promotion, known for its originality and quotability.

ranked_movies.title: Lost in Translation
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a film with beautifully written human moments, focusing on grief, love, awkwardness, and growth without relying on explosions or twists.

ranked_movies.title: Good Will Hunting
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a film filled with authentic human moments and poetry in motion, fitting the request for movies about real people.

ranked_movies.title: The Station Agent
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an early Peter Dinklage role that exemplifies beautiful human moments and authentic storytelling.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Analyzed in-depth in a dedicated thread discussing its themes of laziness, creative obsession, and familial responsibility, demonstrating its lasting impact on film discourse.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Cited for having one of the best comedic moments in a serious scene ('We're leaving') amidst horrifying imagery, showing up in a popular discussion about tone shifts.

ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced for its iconic line of dark comedy in a tense situation ('I'd rather not spend the rest of this winter TIED TO THIS FUCKING COUCH!'), contributing to horror-comedy discussions.

ranked_movies.title: EuroTrip
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in its own thread as an excellent dumb comedy with memorable moments and one of the best movie tie-in songs ('Scotty Doesn't Know'), representing cult comedy recommendations.
